predictions.text: BLS will report CPI-U (headline) inflation at or above 4.0% year-over-year in at least one monthly CPI release.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if any 2026 CPI news release shows CPI-U 12-month percent change >= 4.0% (seasonally adjusted CPI-U level; standard BLS 12-month change table). Otherwise NO.
evidence: Oil surged to $90+ with a ~35% weekly gain, and gasoline prices jumped quickly into inflation expectations (CNBC, 2026-03-06).
Treasury selloff highlights market repricing of inflation vs growth amid the oil shock (MarketWatch, 2026-03-06).
monitoring_signals: AAA national average gasoline and diesel prices
NYMEX RBOB gasoline futures; crack spreads
Monthly CPI energy components (gasoline, fuel oil, utility gas)
Inflation swap/breakeven moves (TIPS)
predictions.rationale: A sustained energy shock often lifts headline CPI rapidly; the question is persistence through at least one CPI reference month by late summer.

predictions.text: University of Michigan Surveys of Consumers will report 1-year inflation expectations at or above 6.0% in either a preliminary or final monthly release.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if any 2026 University of Michigan consumer sentiment release (prelim or final) reports 1-year inflation expectations >= 6.0%. Otherwise NO by deadline.
evidence: Gasoline price jump is feeding quickly into consumer inflation expectations (CNBC, 2026-03-06).
monitoring_signals: UMich preliminary and final releases
Weekly gasoline price averages (AAA/EIA)
Media salience of pump prices and supply disruptions
predictions.rationale: UMich expectations are highly sensitive to gasoline price spikes; current oil move makes a >=6% print plausible within a few months.

predictions.text: The 5-year TIPS breakeven inflation rate (FRED series T5YIE) will close at or above 3.00% on at least one business day.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if FRED series T5YIE (5-Year Breakeven Inflation Rate) shows a value >= 3.00 on any date up to and including the deadline. Otherwise NO.
evidence: Oil shock drove Treasurys’ worst weekly rout in months, reflecting repricing of inflation risk (MarketWatch, 2026-03-06).
monitoring_signals: Daily T5YIE and real yields (DFII5)
WTI/Brent level and volatility
CPI/PCE surprises and inflation expectations surveys
predictions.rationale: Energy-driven inflation risk can re-steepen breakevens; 3% is a high but reachable threshold during acute oil shocks.

predictions.text: ICE Brent crude oil front-month futures will settle at or above $110.00 per barrel on at least one trading day.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if ICE Brent front-month contract official settlement price is >= $110.00/bbl on any trading day by deadline. Otherwise NO.
evidence: Oil surged to $90+ and posted a record weekly gain (~35%) amid Iran war disruption risks (CNBC, 2026-03-06).
Iran war is disrupting oil flows and shipping, raising tail risks for Gulf supply routes (CNBC, 2026-03-06).
monitoring_signals: Strait of Hormuz shipping/security incidents
OPEC+ policy signals and compliance
OECD inventory changes; prompt spreads/backwardation
Brent implied volatility
predictions.rationale: Brent at $110 requires either further escalation or sustained transit disruption; current price momentum makes it possible but still sub-50%.

predictions.text: EIA will report that U.S. total commercial crude oil inventories fell by at least 25.0 million barrels over a rolling 4-week period (week-ending to week-ending).
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report shows 'U.S. ending stocks of crude oil, excluding SPR' (commercial crude) down >= 25.0 million barrels comparing any week-ending value to the value four weeks earlier, for any 4-week window ending by deadline. Otherwise NO.
evidence: Middle East conflict is disrupting oil flows and logistics, potentially tightening balances (CNBC, 2026-03-06).
monitoring_signals: Weekly EIA commercial crude inventories
U.S. crude production, imports, and refinery runs
WTI time spreads and Gulf Coast differentials
Hurricane/maintenance impacts on refining
predictions.rationale: Conflict-driven tightening plus seasonal refinery demand can generate sharp multi-week draws, though supply response could blunt the move.

predictions.text: The U.S. Department of Defense will publicly award at least one single munitions/air-defense-related contract with a total value (base+options or ceiling) of at least $1.5 billion, explicitly citing urgent replenishment/operations needs tied to the Iran conflict or Middle East force protection.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if a DoD contract announcement (Defense.gov, service acquisition site, or official procurement database entry with public description) shows >=$1.5B value and the description links urgency to Iran conflict/Middle East operations/force protection/munitions replenishment. Otherwise NO.
evidence: Trump says defense CEOs will sharply boost 'Exquisite Class' output; defense-tech stocks rallied on wartime demand (CNBC, 2026-03-06).
monitoring_signals: DoD daily contract announcements and congressional notifications
Supplemental funding/reprogramming activity tied to Middle East
Prime contractor earnings calls on urgent orders/backlog
predictions.rationale: Major conflicts typically accelerate large ceiling IDIQs and replenishment buys; explicit linkage in the award text is the key uncertainty.

predictions.text: The U.S. House of Representatives will pass (final passage) at least one War Powers-related bill or joint resolution restricting or conditioning further U.S. military action against Iran, with recorded roll-call passage.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if House Clerk records show final passage by roll-call vote of a War Powers Resolution (or substantially similar measure) that restricts/conditions military action against Iran. Mere introduction, committee action, or a failed vote is insufficient. Otherwise NO.
evidence: Escalating stated U.S. war aims increases congressional pressure to assert authority over use of force (NYT, 2026-03-06).
monitoring_signals: House Rules/Foreign Affairs schedules and privileged resolutions
Leadership statements and whip counts
Bipartisan co-sponsorship levels
predictions.rationale: Roll-call passage requires cross-party alignment and procedural prioritization; plausible amid escalation but historically difficult to clear the House.

predictions.text: At least one of Delta, United, or American will disclose in an SEC filing (10-Q/8-K) or earnings materials an estimated revenue impact of at least $100 million attributable to Middle East route suspensions/disruptions tied to the Iran conflict.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if any of DAL/UAL/AAL quantifies revenue impact (lost revenue, capacity reductions, or similar) >=$100M and ties it to Iran-related Middle East disruption/flight suspensions, in SEC filings or official earnings release/deck/call transcript. Otherwise NO by deadline.
evidence: Iran war is disrupting shipping and regional logistics, raising broader travel/transport disruption risk (CNBC, 2026-03-06).
monitoring_signals: Airline route announcements and schedule filings
10-Q risk factors and MD&A updates
Industry capacity data (OAG/Diio) and load-factor trends
Insurance and rerouting costs
predictions.rationale: Material disruptions often get quantified once carriers report quarterly results; $100M is a high bar unless cancellations persist or rerouting costs surge.

predictions.text: Palantir will be publicly identified as the awardee of a new U.S. DoD or IC contract/task order with an announced value of at least $250 million that explicitly references operational requirements related to the Middle East/Iran conflict (or maritime security in the Gulf).
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if an official award notice (Defense.gov, agency press release, or public procurement record) names Palantir and states value >=$250M, and the description explicitly ties scope to Middle East/Iran-related operational needs (including maritime security in/near the Strait of Hormuz). Otherwise NO.
evidence: Defense-tech stocks and Palantir rallied on wartime demand narrative (CNBC, 2026-03-06).
monitoring_signals: DoD/IC procurement releases and IDIQ task order awards
Palantir investor relations announcements and backlog commentary
Congressional budget line-items for ISR/data fusion/targeting
predictions.rationale: Wartime demand can pull forward large analytics/tasking contracts; explicit Iran/Middle East linkage in public text is the main gating factor.

predictions.text: U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) will issue formal public operational guidance (CSMS message, Federal Register notice, or equivalent) describing a process/timeline for handling court-ordered refunds related to the new global 15% tariff, including any claim submission instructions or interim procedures.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if CBP publishes an official CSMS message or Federal Register/CBP notice that explicitly addresses procedures for refunds/administration tied to the court order involving the global 15% tariff regime. Otherwise NO by deadline.
evidence: CBP says it can’t comply with a refund order; tariff legal battles intensify (CNBC, 2026-03-06).
monitoring_signals: CBP CSMS portal updates and trade notices
DOJ filings seeking stays/clarifications and court deadlines
Importer/trade association bulletins (e.g., NCBFAA) referencing CBP guidance
predictions.rationale: Operational pressure and litigation timelines usually force CBP to publish interim instructions quickly, even if subject to change pending appeal.
